---
title: "Figure S4"
execute:
  echo: false
---

**Figure S4. Time-resolved phylogeny of clade IIIA sequences.** IIIA sequences from the 2018-20202023-2024 outbreak in both MA and NH. Tips are colored by reported residence location (state or MA county). 

```{python}
import os
import baltic as bt
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib as mpl
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib import gridspec
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import matplotlib.font_manager

matplotlib.font_manager.findfont('Calibri')
mpl.rcParams['font.family'] = 'Calibri'
mpl.rcParams['font.sans-serif'] = ['Calibri'] # Specify Calibri for the sans-serif family
mpl.rcParams['font.family'] = 'sans-serif'
mpl.rcParams['font.weight']=200
mpl.rcParams['axes.labelweight']=200
mpl.rcParams['font.size']=22

%matplotlib inline
```

```{python}
OUTPUT_DIR = os.getenv('OUTPUT_DIR')
METADATA_FILE = os.getenv('METADATA_FILE')
RENAMED_NEXUS_FILE = os.getenv('RENAMED_NEXUS_FILE')

OUTPNG = OUTPUT_DIR + "/figS4.png"
OUTPDF = OUTPUT_DIR + "/figS4.pdf"
```

```{python}
# sanity check
meta = pd.read_csv(METADATA_FILE)
meta["county_of_residence"] = meta["county_of_residence"].fillna("")
print(meta["county_of_residence"].unique())

countyColor1 = {"Barnstable County":"#2c2071",
                  "Berkshire County":"#680000",
                  "Bristol County":"#0485e4",
                  "Essex County":"#b4ff06",
                  "Franklin County":"#a90000",
                  "Hampden County":"#ea6800",
                  "Hampshire County":"#de2300",
                  "Middlesex County":"#93d200",
                  "Norfolk County":"#00af9e",
                  "Plymouth County":"#254bc8",
                  "Suffolk County":"#2de000",
                  "Worcester County":"#d1a100",
                  "Dukes County":"#5A2071",
                  "Nantucket County":"#71204B",
                  "NH":"#808080"}
                  #"CT":"#000000"}

meta[meta["county_of_residence"]==""]
# mostly NH samples, a couple nonresident samples from MA
```

```{python}
tree = bt.loadNewick(RENAMED_NEXUS_FILE)
#print([tip.name for tip in tree.getExternal()])
print(len([tip.name for tip in tree.getExternal()])) #266
```

```{python}
tree_tips = set(t.name for t in tree.getExternal()) 
print(len(tree_tips)) # 266
# for now, including the MAVEN NH and CT samples, as well as the NH samples - I decided to set their county_of_residence to "NH" and "CT" respectively
meta.loc[meta["MASPHL_Sequencing_ID"] == "HepA/USA/MA/0212/2019", "county_of_residence"] = "NH"
meta.loc[meta["MASPHL_Sequencing_ID"] == "HepA/USA/MA/0307/2019", "county_of_residence"] = "CT"
metaf = meta[meta["MASPHL_Sequencing_ID"].isin(tree_tips)]

###################
# there aren't any non-QC-passing samples in this set anymore
drop_set = metaf[metaf["Sample_Passed_QC"] != "Yes"]["MASPHL_Sequencing_ID"].tolist()
# also drop the references, MN062167.1 ON524418.1, and outgroup AB279732.1
drop_set.append("MN062167.1")
drop_set.append("ON524418.1")
drop_set.append("AB279732.1")
drop_set.append("HepA/USA/MA/0046/2018") # not outbreak, no county info

# if sample is from NH, make its county of residence "NH"
metaf.loc[metaf["MASPHL_Sequencing_ID"].str.startswith("HepA/USA/NH", na=False), "county_of_residence"] = "NH"

# manually set county of residence for the nonresident MA samples
metaf.loc[metaf["MASPHL_Sequencing_ID"]=="HepA/USA/MA/0307/2019", "county_of_residence"] = "CT" # not in set - IB subgenotype
metaf.loc[metaf["MASPHL_Sequencing_ID"]=="HepA/USA/MA/0212/2019", "county_of_residence"] = "NH" 

print("Tips in drop_set that match tree:", set(drop_set) & tree_tips) # {'HepA/USA/MA/0046/2018'}
print("Tips in drop_set NOT in tree:", set(drop_set) - tree_tips) #none

tips_to_keep = [t for t in tree.getExternal() if t.name not in drop_set]
len(tips_to_keep)
tree = tree.reduceTree(tips_to_keep)
print(len([tip.name for tip in tree.getExternal()]))

# re-filter
metaf = metaf[metaf["MASPHL_Sequencing_ID"].isin([tip.name for tip in tree.getExternal()])]
print(len(metaf))

print(metaf["county_of_residence"].unique())

print(len(tree.root.children)) #2 - tree is rooted
tree.root

len([t for t in tree.getExternal()]) #262
```

```{python}
# treetime outputs decimal year date, stored in num_date annotation
# set absolute time, and add a trait for county color
for t in tree.getExternal():
    if "date" in t.traits:
        t.absoluteTime = t.traits["date"]
        if t.name:      
            subs = metaf.loc[metaf['MASPHL_Sequencing_ID'] == t.name, 'county_of_residence']
            if len(subs)>0:
                testcounty = subs.iloc[0]
                t.traits["county"] = testcounty
                if testcounty in countyColor1.keys():
                    t.traits["county_color"] = countyColor1[testcounty]
                else:
                    print(f"{t.name} {testcounty} not in keys")
            else:
                print(f"{t.name} not in meta")
    else:
        print(f"{t.name} doesn't have date trait")

for t in tree.getInternal():
    if "date" in t.traits:
        t.absoluteTime = t.traits["date"]
```

```{python}
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10, 15), facecolor='w')
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(1, 1, wspace=0.0)
ax = plt.subplot(gs[0], facecolor='w')

x_attr = lambda k: k.absoluteTime
tree.plotTree(ax, x_attr=x_attr, colour='k', linewidth=1)

for tip in tree.getExternal():
    color = tip.traits.get("county_color", "#000000")  # default black jsut in case any are missing
    ax.plot(tip.absoluteTime, tip.y, 'o', color=color, markersize=6)

[ax.spines[loc].set_visible(False) for loc in ax.spines if loc not in ['bottom']]

start_year = int(tree.root.absoluteTime) #traits["num_date"])#absoluteTime)
end_year = int(tree.root.absoluteTime + tree.treeHeight) + 1

# Major (year) lines
for y in range(start_year, end_year):
    ax.axvline(y, color='lightblue', linewidth=1, zorder=-1)
# Minor (year.5) lines
for y in [y + 0.5 for y in range(start_year, end_year)]:
    ax.axvline(y, color='lightgray', linewidth=0.8, linestyle='--', zorder=-1)

x_ticks = list(range(start_year, end_year))
x_labels = [str(y) for y in x_ticks]

ax.set_xticks(x_ticks)
ax.set_xticklabels(x_labels, rotation=0, fontsize=10)

box_width = 0.1
box_height = 1
label_x = tree.root.absoluteTime + tree.treeHeight + 0.2

for tip in tree.getExternal():
    color = tip.traits.get("county_color", "#000000")
    # Dotted line to strip (thinner)
    ax.plot([tip.absoluteTime, label_x + box_width / 2],
            [tip.y, tip.y],
            linestyle=':', color=color, linewidth=1.2)#0.8)
    # Color box
    rect = plt.Rectangle(
        (label_x, tip.y - box_height / 2),
        box_width, box_height,
        facecolor=color,
        edgecolor='none'
    )
    ax.add_patch(rect)
    
# legend
legend_patches = [mpatches.Patch(color=c, label=k) for k, c in countyColor1.items()]
ax.legend(handles=legend_patches, title="MA county",
          bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left',
          borderaxespad=0., frameon=False,
          fontsize=10, title_fontsize=11)  # smaller legend text

# AXIS CLEANUP
ax.tick_params(size=0)
ax.set_yticks([])
ax.set_yticklabels([])
ax.set_xlim(tree.root.absoluteTime - 0.2, tree.root.absoluteTime + tree.treeHeight + 0.5)
ax.set_ylim(-5, tree.ySpan + 5)

fig.savefig(OUTPNG, dpi=300,bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig(OUTPDF, bbox_inches='tight')
# bbox_inches="tight" prevents legend from being clipped (bc my legend extends outside the axes with bbox_to_anchor)
# in some environments, show() can clear the figure - move the saves before plt.show() so it doesn't save blank

plt.show()
```
